comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "landed on main via Gerrit (#143/#144/#163/#164, merged); plan-review is moot for already-merged code — closing to run the completion-verifier gate".

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The two model sub-questions (containment, layer) must be calibrated so suppression never hides a
severe / cross-sibling / delivered-functionality / security finding. Per G-Eval, freeze wording and
calibrate to a gold set (kappa ~0.8) whose anchors are the must-never-suppress cases.

## What

Build a gold-set fixture + an end-to-end partially-complete-epic review test. The gold set encodes
labelled findings: pure re-litigation (drop) vs must-never-suppress anchors — a delivered child's
delivered-functionality/security finding, a finding spanning an open sibling, a finding on a
force-closed child, and a delivered child that opened a security boundary. Add the calibration
record under `docs/calibration/` (a LIVE run, since the changed artifact is a prompt) and the test
module under `tests/plan_review/`.

## Scope

- IN: the gold-set fixture; the end-to-end partially-complete-epic test; the calibration doc.
- OUT: the floor/predicate/sub-call implementations (their own stories).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A gold-set fixture labels drop vs must-never-suppress findings, including the four anchor cases above.
- [ ] An end-to-end test builds a partially-complete epic (delivered + open + force-closed children) and asserts: delivered plan-semantics findings dropped; delivered-functionality/security, cross-sibling, and force-closed-child findings preserved.
- [ ] A calibration record (LIVE LLM run) is written under `docs/calibration/` reporting agreement against the gold set.
- [ ] The suite runs green with the completion floor active and is byte-identical with it inactive.

## Acceptance Criteria (ordering + invariants)

- [ ] Calibration runs only AFTER 94fd's prompt is frozen (explicit `depends_on` 94fd); a later prompt revision retriggers recalibration (the calibration record notes the prompt hash it was run against).
- [ ] A non-regression test asserts the epic's success-criteria invariants: NO `close`-operation change, NO new attestation kind, and code-drift / fire-timing invalidation unchanged (only what a re-fired review DOES changes).

## Acceptance Criteria (gold-set scope)

- [ ] The gold-set has >=5 labelled cases per anchor category (pure re-litigation -> drop; delivered-functionality -> keep; security/contract T5c/T10 -> keep; cross-sibling spans-open -> keep; force-closed child -> keep), >=25 total, and 'complete' = every category represented across the G3/G4, coherence, and overlay criterion provenances.

## Acceptance Criteria (reopen invariant)

- [ ] A test covers the reopened-child auto-resurface invariant: a delivered child whose findings were dropped, once reopened, is no longer `delivered-now` (recomputed that run) so its findings resurface — asserting the recompute-every-run / no-persisted-drop behaviour end to end.

file_impact.reason: end-to-end partially-complete-epic review test + gold-set fixture
live-run calibration record vs the gold set

compiled_state.title: Calibration gold-set + partially-complete-epic tests (must-never-suppress anchors)

verify_commands.dd_text: partially-complete epic: delivered plan-semantics dropped, anchors preserved
